title: rosa spinosissima var. hispida
intro: Rosa spinosissima var. hispida, also known as the Scotch prickly rose, is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its thorny stems and clusters of small, pink or white flowers. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, making it a useful remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Rosa spinosissima var. hispida include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing skin irritations, while also providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
